title: Database handle: create, open, and public API over shard actors
purpose: Deliver the Database handle — the top-level public API that callers actually use. CORE-007 and CORE-008 provide shard actors and the router that maps keys to them, but nothing yet ties those components together behind a single ergonomic entry point. This brief closes that gap. Database::create(config) initialises a new database on disk: it validates the required config fields, creates the directory structure, persists the config, spawns N ShardActors (one per shard), and constructs a ShardRouter over their PIDs. Database::open(path) opens an existing database: it reads the persisted config, restarts each ShardActor from its persisted state with WAL recovery, and rebuilds the router. The public operations — get, put, delete, commit, and range — route through the ShardRouter to the correct shard actor and await the reply. No caller ever touches a ShardActor or ShardRouter directly; the Database is the sole public interface. This is the capstone brief for the core cluster: when it lands, a developer can create a haematite database, write to it, read from it, and rely on crash recovery — without knowing anything about prolly trees, WAL buffers, or shard routing.
task: Create crates/haematite/src/db.rs. Define a DatabaseConfig struct with two required fields: data_dir: PathBuf and shard_count: usize — no optional fields, no defaults (P6, C38). Define a DatabaseError enum covering all failure modes (config missing, invalid shard count, IO errors, shard errors) that implements std::error::Error and Debug. Define a Database struct that holds a ShardRouter and the resolved config (data_dir, shard_count). Implement Database::create(config: DatabaseConfig) -> Result<Database, DatabaseError>: validates that shard_count >= 1, creates the data_dir if absent, persists the config as config.json inside data_dir, spawns shard_count ShardActors (passing each one its data subdirectory and an initial empty root), constructs a ShardRouter from the resulting PIDs, and returns the Database handle. Implement Database::open(path: PathBuf) -> Result<Database, DatabaseError>: reads config.json from path, validates it, restarts shard_count ShardActors from their persisted DurableWal state with WAL recovery, constructs a ShardRouter, and returns the handle. Implement the five public operations — get, put, delete, commit, and range — each routing via ShardRouter to the owning shard actor and awaiting the reply. Implement Drop (or a close() method — see boundaries) to gracefully shut down all shard actors. Wire db.rs into lib.rs: add pub mod db and re-export Database, DatabaseConfig, and DatabaseError from the crate root. Additionally, implement Database::append(key, entries, expected_seq) for Aion's EventStore API (S6, S9): this extends the ShardActor with an Append message type that atomically appends event entries with optimistic concurrency. S7 (read full history) maps to get, and S8 (read from sequence) maps to range — no new operations needed for those stories. This brief does NOT implement branching, merging, cross-shard transactions, read replicas, or dynamic resharding.

requirements.title: DatabaseConfig struct and DatabaseError type
requirements.spec: Define a DatabaseConfig struct with exactly two fields: data_dir: PathBuf and shard_count: usize. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT provide Default for DatabaseConfig — callers must supply both fields explicitly (P6, C38). Define a DatabaseError enum with variants covering: DirectoryCreate(io::Error), ConfigWrite(io::Error), ConfigRead(io::Error), ConfigParse(String), InvalidShardCount, ShardSpawn(String), ShardError(String), and IoError(io::Error). DatabaseConfig SHALL implement Debug and Clone. DatabaseError SHALL implement std::error::Error, Debug, and Display.
acceptance: DatabaseConfig has exactly two fields: data_dir: PathBuf and shard_count: usize — confirmed by grep for 'struct DatabaseConfig' in db.rs
DatabaseConfig does not implement Default — DatabaseConfig::default() fails to compile
DatabaseError implements std::error::Error — confirmed by a #[test] that calls let _: &dyn std::error::Error = &err for each variant
DatabaseError implements Debug and Display — all variants format without panicking
DatabaseError::InvalidShardCount exists and is returned when shard_count == 0
cargo check -p haematite succeeds with DatabaseConfig and DatabaseError defined

requirements.title: Database::create — initialise a new database on disk
requirements.spec: WHEN Database::create(config) is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL validate that config.shard_count >= 1, returning DatabaseError::InvalidShardCount otherwise. THE SYSTEM SHALL create config.data_dir (and any missing parents) if it does not already exist. THE SYSTEM SHALL create one subdirectory per shard named shard-{n} (zero-indexed) inside data_dir for shard actor storage. THE SYSTEM SHALL serialise config to JSON and write it to data_dir/config.json. THE SYSTEM SHALL spawn config.shard_count ShardActors, one per shard subdirectory, with an initial empty root hash. THE SYSTEM SHALL construct a ShardRouter from the resulting PIDs. THE SYSTEM SHALL return a Database holding the router and the resolved config. IF any step fails (directory creation, config write, actor spawn), THE SYSTEM SHALL return a DatabaseError and SHALL NOT leave a partially-initialised database silently accessible.
acceptance: Database::create(config) with shard_count == 0 returns Err(DatabaseError::InvalidShardCount)
Database::create(config) with a valid config creates data_dir on disk
After Database::create, data_dir/config.json exists and contains valid JSON with data_dir and shard_count fields
After Database::create with shard_count == 4, four shard-{0..3} subdirectories exist under data_dir
Database::create returns Ok(Database) when all steps succeed
Database::create with an unwritable data_dir parent returns Err — no partial state is left behind

requirements.title: Database::open — reopen an existing database with WAL recovery
requirements.spec: WHEN Database::open(path) is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL read and parse data_dir/config.json from path, returning DatabaseError::ConfigRead if the file is absent and DatabaseError::ConfigParse if it is malformed. THE SYSTEM SHALL validate that the parsed shard_count >= 1. THE SYSTEM SHALL restart shard_count ShardActors from their persisted shard-{n} subdirectories; each actor calls WalRecovery::recover on startup before entering its message loop, reconstructing any pre-crash buffer state (C36, S5). THE SYSTEM SHALL construct a ShardRouter from the restarted PIDs and return the Database handle. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT reinitialise (overwrite) a shard's DurableWal or committed root on open — open is restoral, not recreation.
acceptance: Database::open(path) with a missing config.json returns Err(DatabaseError::ConfigRead)
Database::open(path) with a malformed config.json returns Err(DatabaseError::ConfigParse)
Database::open(path) for a database created with shard_count == 4 restarts exactly 4 ShardActors
After Database::open, a key that was put-and-committed before the process exited is retrievable via get — confirming tree state persisted
After a simulated crash followed by Database::open, a key that was put-to-WAL but not committed before the crash is retrievable via get — confirming WAL replay reconstructed the buffer
Database::open does not overwrite or truncate any existing DurableWal or node store file

requirements.title: Public get, put, delete operations routed via ShardRouter
requirements.spec: WHEN Database::get(key: &[u8]) is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL route to the shard actor at ShardRouter::pid_for(key), send ShardMessage::Get, and return the result as Result<Option<Vec<u8>>, DatabaseError>. WHEN Database::put(key: Vec<u8>, value: Vec<u8>) is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL route to the owning shard actor, send ShardMessage::Put, await ShardReply::Ack, and return Result<(), DatabaseError>. WHEN Database::delete(key: Vec<u8>) is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL route to the owning shard actor, send ShardMessage::Delete, await ShardReply::Ack, and return Result<(), DatabaseError>. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT send a get, put, or delete to any shard other than the one determined by ShardRouter for that key. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT expose ShardMessage, ShardReply, ShardRouter, or ShardActor in the return types of these methods — callers interact with DatabaseError only.
acceptance: Database::get(key) returns Ok(None) for a key that was never written
Database::get(key) returns Ok(Some(value)) immediately after Database::put(key, value) — even before commit
Database::put(key, value) returns Ok(()) on success
Database::delete(key) followed by Database::get(key) returns Ok(None) — the buffer shadows the deletion
A put and a get for the same key always route to the same shard — confirmed by checking shard_for(key) returns the same index each time
The return types of get, put, and delete do not reference ShardMessage, ShardReply, ShardRouter, or beamr::Pid

requirements.title: Database::commit — flush all shard buffers atomically
requirements.spec: WHEN Database::commit() is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL send ShardMessage::Commit to every shard actor in parallel, await all ShardReply::Committed(hash) responses, and return Result<Vec<Hash>, DatabaseError> where the Vec contains one committed root hash per shard in shard-index order. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT commit only a subset of shards — commit is an all-shards operation. IF any shard returns an error, THE SYSTEM SHALL return DatabaseError::ShardError for that failure; remaining committed hashes are not returned. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT block other shards while awaiting a slow shard's commit — sends are parallel, not serial.
acceptance: Database::commit() after N puts returns Ok(hashes) with hashes.len() == shard_count
Database::commit() on an empty (no buffered writes) database returns Ok(hashes) — committing zero mutations is valid
The hash at index i in the result corresponds to shard i — i.e. hashes[ShardRouter::shard_for(key)] is the new root hash for the shard that owns key
A test confirms commit sends to all shards in parallel rather than sequentially — measured by checking that two shards' commit handlers are invoked concurrently
After commit, a subsequent put-then-get still returns the new value (buffer still operational post-commit)
If one shard's commit fails, Database::commit() returns Err(DatabaseError::ShardError)

requirements.title: Database::range — sorted key scan routed to the owning shard
requirements.spec: WHEN Database::range(from: &[u8], to: &[u8]) is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL route the range query to the single shard actor that owns keys in [from, to) — determined by ShardRouter::shard_for(from). THE SYSTEM SHALL send ShardMessage::Range to that shard actor, collect all ShardReply::RangeEntry { key, value } replies until ShardReply::RangeDone, and return them as Result<Vec<(Vec<u8>, Vec<u8>)>, DatabaseError> in ascending key order. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT contact multiple shard actors for a single range call — for this release, range is a single-shard operation. IF from >= to, THE SYSTEM SHALL return Ok(vec![]) — an empty range is not an error.
acceptance: Database::range(from, to) where from >= to returns Ok(vec![])
Database::range(from, to) returns all (key, value) pairs with from <= key < to in ascending key order
Database::range includes buffered (uncommitted) entries: keys put but not yet committed appear in results
A deleted key does not appear in Database::range results
Database::range routes only to a single shard actor — confirmed by checking that only one ShardMessage::Range is sent per call
Database::range returns Ok(vec![]) when no keys fall in the requested range

requirements.title: Database shutdown via Drop
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL implement Drop for Database such that dropping the handle sends a graceful shutdown signal to all shard actors, allowing them to flush any in-flight state before the process exits. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT use unwrap() or expect() in the Drop implementation — if shutdown message delivery fails (e.g. the actor already crashed), Drop SHALL log or ignore the failure silently rather than panicking. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT expose a separate close() method as the public API — Drop is the single shutdown path.
acceptance: Database implements Drop — confirmed by grep for 'impl Drop for Database' in db.rs
Dropping a Database handle does not panic, even if a shard actor has already crashed
A test creates a Database, writes to it, then drops it — no panic occurs and all shard PIDs are no longer responsive after the drop
Drop does not call unwrap() or expect() — confirmed by grep for 'unwrap()\|expect(' in the Drop impl block

requirements.title: Wire Database into crate root and end-to-end integration test
requirements.spec: Add pub mod db to crates/haematite/src/lib.rs and re-export Database, DatabaseConfig, and DatabaseError from the crate root. THE SYSTEM SHALL include an integration test in db.rs (under #[cfg(test)]) that: creates a Database with shard_count == 4 in a temp directory, puts 100 key-value pairs, calls commit, calls get on each key and asserts the value matches, calls range over the full key space and asserts 100 results are returned in sorted order, drops the Database, re-opens it with Database::open, and asserts the same 100 keys are still retrievable. This test proves the full create-write-commit-close-open-read cycle (S1 through S5).
acceptance: haematite::Database, haematite::DatabaseConfig, and haematite::DatabaseError are importable from the crate root after wiring
The integration test creates a Database with shard_count == 4 and puts 100 key-value pairs without error
The integration test calls commit and receives Ok(hashes) with hashes.len() == 4
The integration test calls get for each of the 100 keys and receives the correct value
The integration test calls range and receives exactly 100 entries in ascending key order
The integration test drops the Database, re-opens it with Database::open, and retrieves all 100 keys correctly — confirming persistence
cargo test -p haematite --lib db passes with no failures

requirements.title: Database::append — optimistic concurrency append for event sourcing
requirements.spec: WHEN Database::append(key, entries, expected_seq) is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL route to the owning shard actor, send an Append message containing the key, a Vec of value entries, and the expected sequence number. THE SYSTEM SHALL atomically append all entries if the current sequence for that key matches expected_seq, returning the new sequence number. IF the current sequence does not match expected_seq, THE SYSTEM SHALL return Err(DatabaseError::SequenceConflict { expected, actual }) without modifying any data. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT allow partial appends — either all entries are appended or none are. WHEN multiple entries are appended in one call, THE SYSTEM SHALL produce exactly one WAL commit for the batch (CN8). This operation maps to Aion's EventStore append API: S6 (optimistic concurrency), S9 (atomic batch append).
acceptance: Database::append(key, entries, 0) on a fresh key returns Ok(new_seq) where new_seq == entries.len()
Database::append(key, entries, wrong_seq) returns Err(DatabaseError::SequenceConflict { expected: wrong_seq, actual: current_seq })
Two concurrent appends to the same key with the same expected_seq: one succeeds, the other returns SequenceConflict
Database::append with 3 entries produces exactly one WAL write — not 3 individual writes
After Database::append, Database::get(key) returns the concatenated event history including the new entries
Database::append(key, vec![], expected_seq) with empty entries is a no-op that returns Ok(expected_seq)

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement branching, forking, or merging operations — those are covered by the branching cluster
SHALL NOT implement cross-shard transactions or two-phase commit — serialisability is per-shard only, as specified in design non-goals
SHALL NOT implement read replicas or secondary shard processes — deferred to a future phase
SHALL NOT implement dynamic resharding or allow shard_count to change after Database::create — ADR-004 fixes shard count at creation
SHALL NOT implement secondary indexes or query planning — scan with predicate covers current needs per design non-goals
SHALL NOT expose ShardActor, ShardMessage, ShardReply, or ShardRouter in the public API surface — Database is the sole entry point
SHALL NOT add silent defaults to DatabaseConfig — both data_dir and shard_count are required and must be supplied by the caller (P6)
verification: cargo check -p haematite succeeds with no errors
cargo test -p haematite --lib passes all tests including the end-to-end integration test in db.rs
cargo clippy -p haematite -- -D warnings produces no warnings
grep -rn 'unwrap()\|expect(' crates/haematite/src/db.rs produces no matches outside #[cfg(test)] blocks
grep -n 'impl Default for DatabaseConfig' crates/haematite/src/db.rs returns no matches — DatabaseConfig has no Default impl
haematite::Database, haematite::DatabaseConfig, and haematite::DatabaseError are importable from the crate root
A round-trip test: Database::create, put 10 keys, commit, drop, Database::open, get all 10 keys — each returns the correct value
